USA.notes: Tipping is expected in restaurants, taxis, and for personal services. 18% is standard for sit-down meals; 20% for excellent service. Bartenders: $1-2/drink. Hotel housekeeping: $2-5/night.

UK.notes: Service charge often added automatically (12.5%). If not, 10% is appreciated in restaurants. Pubs: tipping not customary unless table service. Taxis: round up the fare.

Canada.notes: Similar to the USA. 15% is standard, 18-20% for good service. Hairdressers and spa staff: 15-20%. Hotel staff: $2-5 CAD per service.

France.notes: Service is included by law ("service compris"). Leaving 5-10% extra for good service is appreciated but never expected. Round up taxi fares.

Italy.notes: A "coperto" cover charge is common (€1-3 per person). Tipping is not expected; rounding up or leaving a few euros for excellent service is enough.

Spain.notes: Tipping is modest. Leave small change at cafés, 5-10% in restaurants for good service. Not expected at tapas bars.

Germany.notes: Round up the bill or add 5-10%. Tell the server the total amount including tip when paying — don't leave money on the table.

Japan.notes: Tipping is not customary and can be considered rude. Excellent service is the standard. High-end ryokans accept gratuities only when given discreetly in an envelope.

Pakistan.notes: Tipping ("baksheesh") is appreciated. Rs 100-200 for a meal at mid-range restaurants; round up taxis. Hotel porters: Rs 50-100 per bag.

UAE.notes: Service charge (10%) often added but rarely reaches staff. Add 10-15% in cash for good service. Valet, porters, drivers: AED 5-10 each.

Turkey.notes: 10% is standard in restaurants, 15% for excellent service. Tip in cash where possible. Round up taxi fares; tip hotel porters 20-50 TRY per bag.

Thailand.notes: Not traditionally expected but increasingly common in tourist areas. 10% in restaurants without service charge. Round up taxis. Hotel porters: 20-50 THB per bag.

Mexico.notes: 15% is standard in restaurants. Bartenders: 10-20 MXN per drink. Hotel housekeeping: 20-50 MXN per night. Tour guides expect a tip at the end.

Greece.notes: Service charge is sometimes included; if not, 5-10% is generous. Round up taxis. Café staff: leaving small change is enough.
